name: Gorafe Desert Trail
name_original: Desierto de Los Coloraos — circular desde Gorafe — Badlands Castilléjar y Galera
description: Explore the moderate Desierto de Los Coloraos mountain biking trails near Gorafe, covering 15.9 miles through unique badlands.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Gorafe offers routes through a unique "badlands" desert landscape within the Granada Geopark. The region is characterized by dramatic canyons, rolling hills, and distinctive rock formations sculpted by erosion. Geological features include large gullies and colorful sandstone formations, notably the red-colored mountains of "Los Coloraos." The terrain consists of dirt trails, dry riverbeds, and canyon paths, providing varied riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Gorafe The most popular mountain bike trail is Gorafe Desert Trail, a 15.9 miles (25.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route explores the unique desert landscape with its distinctive geological formations. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Los Colorados – circular desde Villanueva de las Torres por el Desierto de Gorafe, a moderate 19.1 miles (30.7 km) path. This route traverses the striking red-colored mountains and deep canyons of Los Coloraos. Local mountain bikers also love the Acequia del Toril and dolmens of Alicún - circular route through Fonelas, a 22.6 miles (36.4 km) trail leading through areas with ancient megalithic sites, often completed in about 3 hours 0 minutes. Mountain biking around Gorafe is defined by badlands, dramatic canyons, and ancient megalithic sites. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Gorafe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Gorafe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Gorafe?
A: There are 24 mountain bike trails available around Gorafe, offering a range of experiences through the unique badlands landscape of the Granada Geopark.

Q: What is the terrain like for mountain biking in Gorafe?
A: The terrain in Gorafe is characterized by a unique 'badlands' desert landscape, featuring rolling hills, ravines, and dramatic rock formations. You'll encounter dirt trails, ramblas (dry riverbeds), and canyon paths, with some sections having loose stones. The area includes striking geological features like large gullies and colorful sandstone formations, particularly the red-colored mountains of 'Los Coloraos'.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly mountain bike routes in Gorafe?
A: While Gorafe's trails are generally rated as moderate or difficult, requiring basic confidence in handling a bicycle, there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' for beginners in the komoot guide. Most routes navigate varied terrain with some technical sections.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Gorafe?
A: Mountain biking in Gorafe offers stunning views of the unique badlands landscape. You can explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorafe Desert&lt;/a&gt;, the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands&lt;/a&gt; with their red-colored mountains, and various canyons. Keep an eye out for viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5520220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the sculpted landscape.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Gorafe?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Gorafe are circular. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155646931/los-colorados-circular-desde-villanueva-de-las-torres-por-el-desierto-de-gorafe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Colorados – circular desde Villanueva de las Torres por el Desierto de Gorafe&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the striking red mountains, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155320759/acequia-del-toril-and-dolmens-of-alicun-circular-route-through-fonelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia del Toril and dolmens of Alicún - circular route through Fonelas&lt;/a&gt; explores areas with ancient megalithic sites.

Q: What is the best time of year to mountain bike in Gorafe?
A: Gorafe's desert climate means that spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant times for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and while winters are mild, some days might be cooler. Checking local weather conditions before your ride is always recommended.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Gorafe?
A: The mountain bike trails in Gorafe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique 'lunar landscape' and the dramatic geological formations, especially the vibrant colors of 'Los Coloraos' at sunrise or sunset.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical or archaeological sites?
A: Yes, Gorafe is home to the largest concentration of dolmens in Spain. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155320759/acequia-del-toril-and-dolmens-of-alicun-circular-route-through-fonelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia del Toril and dolmens of Alicún - circular route through Fonelas&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through areas where you can encounter these ancient megalithic burial structures, adding a historical dimension to your ride.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that offer challenging climbs or descents?
A: Yes, Gorafe offers routes with significant elevation changes. For a more challenging experience, consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands – Gorafe Desert Canyons loop from Gorafe&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 800 meters of elevation gain and descent, traversing the dramatic canyon landscapes.

Q: Are there any services for mountain bike rentals or guided tours in Gorafe?
A: Yes, services offering mountain bike, gravel bike, and e-bike rentals are available in the Gorafe area. You can also find guided tours that explore the canyons, badlands, and megalithic sites, providing an opportunity to discover the region with local expertise.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Gorafe?
A: The longest mountain bike trail in this guide is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Coloraos Badlands – Gorafe Desert Canyons loop from Gorafe&lt;/a&gt;, covering a distance of approximately 47.8 kilometers (29.7 miles).
tips:
If you're hiking the Gorafe desert trail counterclockwise, this is the first spectacular point. 
Enjoy the fantastical forms, carved by wind and water into towers and walls.
If it's raining or has recently rained, don't even think about going down the ravines: it's impossible to pedal on such sticky clay.
This beautiful place located in Gorafe owes its name to the predominant color in the geological formations of its clay soils.
From here the Desierto Gorafe lies at your feet. Great panoramas at any time of the day or year. Absolute must on gravel and mountain bike tours.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily traverses unpaved surfaces, including gravel and natural paths, with some singletrack sections. You'll experience the unique 'badlands' formations, characterized by extensive networks of arid desert landscapes, dramatic geological features, and the striking sandstone rock formations known as 'Los Coloraos'.

faqs.question: How difficult is this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as a moderate mountain bike ride, requiring good fitness. While some segments are suitable for all skill levels (STS S0-S1), other portions demand advanced riding skills (STS S2). More challenging sections may require very good fitness and potentially pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning views and unique geological formations. Key highlights include the Cerro de la Bandera, the dramatic Gorafe Desert Canyons, the visually striking Los Coloraos Badlands, and the panoramic Puntal de Don Diego Viewpoint.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The 'red deserts' of Los Coloraos offer great panoramas at any time of the day or year. However, for mountain biking, spring and autumn are generally ideal to avoid the extreme heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in this region, it's important to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and respect other trail users. Be mindful of the arid conditions and ensure your dog has plenty of water, especially during warmer months, as there are no natural water sources on the trail.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Geoparque de Granada. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on the public trails within the Geoparque. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail near Gorafe?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Gorafe. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town of Gorafe, which serves as a convenient starting point for this loop. Specific parking areas may vary, so look for designated spots or consult local maps upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route overlaps with several other paths. You will find sections that are part of the Ruta del Desierto de Gorafe, Gorafe - Vereda Chirlata, El Camino Espiritual del Sur, and various segments of the Camino de los Campos Mones.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near Gorafe, a small desert town. You can find basic amenities, including cafes and potentially some local accommodation, in Gorafe itself. The nearby towns of Castilléjar and Galera also offer additional options for refreshments and lodging.

faqs.question: What makes this trail unique compared to other mountain biking routes in Granada?
faqs.answer: This trail is celebrated as one of the most beautiful mountain bike routes in Granada due to its unique desert landscapes and dramatic badlands formations, particularly the visually striking 'Los Coloraos'. It offers an immersive experience in the wild and vast deserts of Southern Spain, providing a glimpse into a truly unique part of the world.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and sections requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills (STS S2), this trail might be challenging for younger children or families new to mountain biking. Some easier segments (STS S0-S1) could be suitable, but the full loop requires a higher level of experience and endurance.
